rxn_class_summary: Radical-mediated three-component difunctionalization of alkenes with diazo compounds, delivering hydroalkylation (via photoredox/thiol HAT) or azidoalkylation (via Fe catalysis with TMSN3/TBHP), bypassing cyclopropanation.

key_intermediates.name: Ru(I) reductant generated after oxidation of Hantzsch ester
alpha-ester carbon radical I (•CH(CO2Et)R) from diazo after loss of N2 and protonation
benzylic alkyl radical II after addition to styrene
Fe(II)X2 / Fe(III)X2N3 redox couple in azidoalkylation
tBuO• / tBuOO• radicals from Fe + TBHP
α-azido benzyl radical 12-C (from addition to α-phenyl vinyl azide)
iminyl radical 12-N after loss of N2 from 12-C

electron_flow: For hydroalkylation: photoexcited Ru(II)* oxidizes Hantzsch ester (PCET) generating Ru(I) and 3a'; Ru(I) reduces the diazo compound, which loses N2 and is protonated to give α-ester radical I that adds to the alkene to form radical II; thiol-mediated HAT from 3a' delivers the C–H bond of product. For azidoalkylation: Fe(II)/TBHP generates tBuO•/Fe(III)X2OR, which reduces the diazo via PCET to radical I; addition to alkene gives radical II; Fe(III)X2N3 (formed by ligand exchange with HN3 from TMSN3) transfers N3 to radical II, regenerating Fe(II).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 3.0 equiv TEMPO fully suppresses both hydroalkylation (4a: 97%→0%) and azidoalkylation (6a: 73%→0%), consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: α-cyclopropylstyrene (radical clock 8)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened products 9 (88%, E:Z 90:10) and 10/10' observed, confirming radical addition/ring-opening.

probe_reagents.name: D2O (6.5 equiv) in MeCN
probe_reagents.observation: Dideuterated product 4a' formed (D at α and γ positions), indicating water/protic solvent provides H atoms in the photocatalytic system.

probe_reagents.name: 2-propanol-d8 solvent
probe_reagents.observation: In azidoalkylation, mixture of 6a/6a'/6a'' (8:37:55) indicates partial deuterium incorporation; H also from TBHP/H2O.

probe_reagents.name: preformed cyclopropane 11 (from carbene + 1,1-diphenylethylene)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 11 does NOT open to give 4bh or 6q under either set of conditions, ruling out a cyclopropane intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: α-phenyl vinyl azide 1cr
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping gives ketone 12 (47%) via iminyl radical 12-N (confirmed by GC-MS of imine 12'), supporting the radical addition pathway; requires light and photocatalyst but not thiol.

probe_reagents.name: Control: omission of Ru(bpy)3Cl2, light, or thiol
probe_reagents.observation: No or trace product without photocatalyst, light, or Hantzsch ester; replacing Ru with Eosin Y fails – establishes role of each component.

notes: Two complementary catalytic manifolds share a common α-ester carbon radical generated from the diazo via PCET (Ru(I)-mediated for hydroalkylation; Fe(II)/TBHP-mediated for azidoalkylation). Authors explicitly exclude a free-carbene/cyclopropane pathway (preformed cyclopropane does not open). For azidoalkylation, azide delivery is proposed via inner-sphere ligand transfer from Fe(III)X2N3 to alkyl radical II rather than free N3• addition.
